content.title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
content.summary: OpenAI launches Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative with Trail of Bits, HackerOne, and Calif to help open-source maintainers find and fix vulnerabilities using AI-assisted security research.
content.summary_1: OpenAI has introduced Patch the Planet under its Daybreak cybersecurity program, partnering with Trail of Bits to deploy AI-assisted vulnerability discovery and patch development across major open-source projects. Using GPT-5.5-Cyber and Codex Security, the initiative targets critical projects including cURL, Python, Go, Sigstore, and pyca/cryptography. In an initial five-day sprint, researchers discovered hundreds of security issues, merged dozens of patches, and found exploitable vulnerabilities in Chrome V8, Safari WebKit, Firefox, OpenBSD, FreeBSD, and Linux kernel. Every finding is reviewed by human security engineers before reaching maintainers, reducing false-positive noise. The goal is to move beyond vulnerability discovery toward a complete patch automation loop.
content.body_text:
OpenAI has introduced Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative built with Trail of Bits to help open-source maintainers strengthen critical software the world depends on. Pairing AI-assisted security research (GPT-5.5-Cyber, Codex Security) with expert human review, the program not only identifies vulnerabilities but helps develop and deploy patches.

Initial participants include cURL, NATS Server, pyca/cryptography, Sigstore, aiohttp, Go, freenginx, Python, and python.org. In the initial sprint, Trail of Bits engineers working full-time found:
- Linux Kernel: 8 pointer information leak PoCs and 24 local privilege escalation exploits auto-generated
- OpenBSD: A 23-year-old use-after-free in kernel System V semaphores
- FreeBSD: 34 confirmed vulnerabilities, 7 local privilege escalation PoCs
- dnsmasq: 4 of 6 CVEs independently found before disclosure
- HTTP/2 Bomb: DoS affecting NGINX, Apache, IIS, Pingora – 880,000+ websites affected
- Chrome V8: 5 exploitable vulnerabilities
- Safari WebKit: 10+ exploitable vulnerabilities
- Firefox: WebAssembly CVE-2026-8390 found and patched 2 days before Pwn2Own Berlin

Key innovation: every finding is manually reviewed by Trail of Bits security engineers before reaching maintainers, preventing false-positive overload. The program also builds reusable fuzzing harnesses, CVE-variant pipelines, and differential testing infrastructure.

tech_insights.impact_summary: OpenAI deploys frontier AI models to discover and patch critical vulnerabilities in foundational open-source software at unprecedented scale and speed.
actions_developer: Open-source maintainers can apply to join Patch the Planet at trailofbits.com/patch-the-planet
Explore Codex Security plugin for automated vulnerability scanning and patching in your own codebases
Review GPT-5.5-Cyber benchmark results (85.6% CyberGym) when evaluating AI security tools
actions_business: Security teams: evaluate Daybreak Cyber Partner Program for enterprise vulnerability management
Consider AI-assisted security workflows that pair model discovery with human expert review to reduce false-positive burden
Monitor coordinated disclosure timelines for affected open-source dependencies (cURL, Python, Go, etc.)
